Check possible hands: we have a pair of Qs (H_Q, C_Q). Also pair of 5s (H_5, D_5). Could we make two pair? Need two pairs of different ranks: we have Q pair and 5 pair, plus extra card. Four of a Kind? no.

We have 4 hands left. Score target 300, need 3*100 = 300. So three two-pair hands would be enough. But we have only one pair of Q and one pair of 5. After playing those cards, they are used and replaced with new draws. We can aim to make more two pair later.

We must ensure indices after selection reflect original positions. They are 0,1,3,4,5? Wait we need both 5s: indices 4 (H_5) and 5 (D_5). So choose indices [0,1,3,4,5]? That's Q_H, Q_C, S_6, H_5, D_5. That's fine.

Let's play that.

3: H_6 (Six of Hearts)
4: face down
5: face down
6: face down
7: S_2 (Two of Spades)

We have 1 Joker: Even Steven (even rank give +4 Mult). Sell value $2.

Money $2. Hands left 3/4, discards left 2/4. Target 1600, current score 260.

We need to reach 1600.

We have 3 hands left to play (max 5 cards each). Also we have 2 discards left (each discarding up to 5 cards). We need to get at least 1340 more points.

Even Steven gives +4 Mult per even-ranked card in the hand when those cards are scored. Even ranks are 2,4,6,8,10. So we have 10 (C_T), 6 (S_6, H_6), 2 (S_2). That's four even cards.

We need to form a high scoring hand. Best possible hand is Straight Flush or Four of a Kind etc. But we have limited cards.

We have face-down cards (unknown). First hand we play will be drawn face-down (because The House blind). That means the hand we play first (i.e., when we select cards to play) will be hidden; we cannot see its composition? Actually The House: First hand is drawn face down. So the first hand we play will be unknown to us, but we can still play cards. But scoring still works; we just don't know the hand type until after playing. But we can try to play many cards to maximize chance of high hand.

Given we have 3 hands left, the first hand is face-down, the others will be visible. So maybe we want to use the first hand to cycle cards and get better hand for second/third.

We can discard now (2 discards left) to try to get better cards.

We have 8 cards; we can discard up to 5. Maybe discard low-value odd cards like H_J (Jack rank 10? Actually Jack is rank 10? In scoring, face cards count as 10. Even? Jack is 10 (even). Actually rank is considered value: Jack=10, which is even. So Even Steven triggers on even rank cards: includes 10, so J counts as even? Yes rank 10 is even. So Jack is even rank, gives +4 Mult.

But Joker effect adds +4 Mult per even rank when scored. So each even card in hand that is part of scoring hand gets +4 Mult.

Thus many cards are even: 10, 6,6,2, maybe face-down cards unknown.

Goal: get a hand with many even cards, maybe Four of a Kind (4 same rank) which could be evens.

We have two 6s; if we can get two more 6s from draws, we could have Four of a Kind 6s. That would score base chips 60, base mult 7, plus Even Steven: 4 even cards (the four 6s) each +4 = +16 mult. So total mult = 7+16=23. Score = (chips 60 + cards value sum). Card values sum: each 6 counts as 6 (value 6). Four 6s total 24. So chips = 60+24=84. Score = 84 * 23 = 1932, which would exceed target in one hand. Good.
